data.title: Sprinklr outlines $825M–$827M FY26 revenue target while expanding AI platform and launching $150M buyback
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Sprinklr, Inc. (CXM) Q1 2026

Management View

  • President and CEO Rory P. Read reported "first quarter total revenue grew 5% year-over-year to $205.5 million, and subscription revenue grew 4% year-over-year to $184.1 million." He highlighted $36.7 million in non-GAAP operating income, an 18% non-GAAP operating margin, and a record $81 million in free cash flow for the quarter. Read described FY '26 as a transitional year, emphasizing ongoing transformation efforts including a "clear ambidextrous strategy, implemented a business management system, optimized our cost structure, realigned our go-to-market coverage model and strengthened our product delivery road maps." He acknowledged "significant work to do across our business to elevate the consistency of our execution, improve the predictability of our results and drive future growth."
  • Read announced the appointment of Sanjay Macwan as Chief Information Officer and suggested that "we will make further leadership team additions as we move through FY '26." He detailed engagement initiatives like Project Bear Hug, targeting the top 500 customers, and shared customer wins such as Calvin Klein, LG Electronics, and Pepsi. Read also noted industry recognition from Gartner and Forrester for the company's cross-product innovation and unified platform.
  • Chief Financial Officer Manish Sarin stated, "For the first quarter, total revenue was $205.5 million, up 5% year-over-year, while subscription revenue was $184.1 million, up 4% year-over-year. Professional services revenue came in at $21.4 million." Sarin highlighted a subscription revenue-based net dollar expansion rate of 102% and noted 146 customers generating at least $1 million in annual subscription revenue. He also announced, "Sprinklr's Board has authorized a new $150 million stock buyback program, and we intend to complete the full buyback by June 30, 2026."

Outlook

  • Sarin guided Q2 total revenue between $205 million and $206 million, with subscription revenue between $184 million and $185 million and professional services revenue of $21 million. For the full year FY '26, he maintained subscription revenue guidance of $741 million to $743 million and increased total revenue guidance to a range of $825 million to $827 million, citing "a $3.5 million increase from prior guidance, driven by an increase in our professional services revenue expectation to $84 million."
  • Sarin projected non-GAAP operating income for FY '26 in the range of $129 million to $131 million, with a non-GAAP net income per diluted share of $0.39 to $0.40 and a 16% non-GAAP operating margin at the midpoint. Free cash flow for FY '26 is expected at approximately $125 million, tracking to a 15% margin.
  • Management acknowledged macroeconomic uncertainty, noting "customers to be more cautious and scrutinize their spending decisions for the remainder of the year."

Financial Results

  • Sarin reported a non-GAAP operating income of $36.7 million, non-GAAP net income of $0.12 per diluted share, and $80.7 million in free cash flow, with an adjusted figure of $92.5 million factoring in restructuring cash outflows. The balance sheet showed $570.2 million in cash and marketable securities with no debt outstanding.
  • The quarter included $16.3 million in restructuring charges and $0.8 million in litigation costs, both excluded from non-GAAP figures. Calculated billings reached $204.3 million, total RPO was $943.2 million, and CRPO was $596.8 million.
  • Non-GAAP subscription gross margin was 78%, professional services gross margin was 6%, and total non-GAAP gross margin was 70%.

Q&A

  • Arjun Rohit Bhatia, William Blair: Asked about go-to-market transformation timeline and progress. Read responded that "FY '26 would be a transitional year," aiming for momentum in 3Q and 4Q, with Project Bear Hug targeting 500–600 customers over two quarters. Bhatia inquired about differentiation in CCaaS; Read emphasized AI capabilities and the unified platform, but noted the need to "harden" the solution before accelerating further expansion.
  • Pinjalim Bora, JPMorgan: Questioned sales cycle elongation and logo churn. Read said that scrutiny is broad-based but more related to execution than geography or vertical. He described renewal pressure as a long-term issue, with improvement expected as implementation consistency grows. Bora also asked about churn among product lines; Read indicated little variation and referenced Project Tiger Shark for core innovation.
  • Jackson Edmund Ader, KeyBanc: Asked about criteria for Bear Hug customers and FX headwind mitigation. Read explained a focus on largest accounts and analytics-driven health checks. Sarin said all spending is under review to offset the $10 million FX headwind. Read added prioritization of investments in AI, enablement, and technical capability.
  • Raimo Lenschow, Barclays: Inquired about AI’s impact on CCaaS. Read described AI as "100% real" and disruptive, requiring maturation of Sprinklr into a "mission-critical unified AI native customer experience platform." Lenschow asked about the scope of the business management system; Sarin emphasized its comprehensive nature across product, sales, and enablement.
  • Elizabeth Porter, Morgan Stanley: Asked about sales culture changes and metrics. Read described the pod structure, new incentives, and the importance of collaboration and technical capability. Porter also queried headcount outlook post-restructuring; Read suggested minimal change, with targeted investments in technical roles.
  • Patrick D. Walravens, Citizens JMP: Asked about strategic value and transformation pace. Read outlined a phased approach, with business optimization largely complete and acceleration expected post-transition year. Walravens also asked about dollar expansion; Sarin sees the rate stabilizing near 102%.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, acknowledging progress but frequently probing for clarity on execution timelines, churn drivers, and transformation milestones. Questions often focused on renewal risk, sales transformation, and macro impacts.
  • Management’s tone in prepared remarks was pragmatic and forward-looking, emphasizing transformation and acknowledging challenges. In the Q&A, Read’s responses were candid about the need for improvement, frequently referencing the transitional nature of FY '26 and the ongoing work required. Confidence was tempered with repeated reminders that results would take time, using phrases such as "work in progress" and "we have significant work to do."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a constructive but cautious tone, with continued focus on execution challenges and transformation steps. Management’s confidence remained steady but realistic about the timeline and hurdles.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for total FY '26 revenue was raised from $821.5–$823.5 million to $825–$827 million, reflecting increased professional services expectations. Subscription revenue guidance was maintained at $741–$743 million. Q1 total revenue and subscription revenue both increased sequentially from Q4.
  • Non-GAAP operating income margin improved from 13% in Q4 to 18% in Q1. Free cash flow saw a significant jump from $1.5 million in Q4 to $80.7 million in Q1, with an adjusted figure of $92.5 million.
  • The non-GAAP subscription gross margin decreased slightly from 79% to 78%, and the net dollar expansion rate dropped from 104% to 102%, reflecting ongoing churn and downsell pressures.
  • Management’s tone continued to recognize challenges but highlighted progress in operating efficiency, customer engagement, and leadership additions. Analyst questions consistently centered on renewal stability, sales transformation, and macro uncertainty, similar to the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Read identified inconsistent operational execution, technical debt, longer sales cycles, and increased scrutiny of enterprise spending as ongoing challenges. He noted that "addressing all forms of churn is a top priority in our transformational journey."
  • Sarin warned of a $10 million negative FX impact on non-GAAP operating expenses but expressed confidence in offsetting these costs through savings.
  • Analysts raised concerns about renewal pressure, churn, the timing of transformational benefits, and effectiveness of the new go-to-market model. Management emphasized ongoing initiatives to address these risks, including Project Bear Hug, sales enablement, and continued investment in product innovation and customer engagement.

Final Takeaway

Sprinklr’s first quarter of FY 2026 demonstrated progress in its transformation strategy, with sequential improvements in both revenue and operating margin, record free cash flow, and the launch of a $150 million buyback program. Management reaffirmed its full-year guidance while highlighting ongoing challenges in customer renewals, execution consistency, and macroeconomic uncertainty. The company is investing in leadership, sales transformation, and innovation to position itself for durable growth, with a focus on strengthening customer relationships and expanding its AI-native platform—expecting tangible results to materialize in the second half of the year and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: DocuSign Q1 result on deck: IAM updates and subscription revenue growth in focus
data.description:
Software company DocuSign (NASDAQ:DOCU) is set to announce the first quarter earnings on Thursday, and investors will look forward to updates on Intelligent Agreement Management (IAM) suite and subscription revenue growth.

The artificial intelligence-enabled content Intelligent Agreement Management, or IAM, is a cloud software repository to centrally store, manage, and analyze agreements across an organization, unlocking data trapped in agreements to drive efficiencies, uncover opportunities to lower costs, and reduce risk, according to the company.

Wall Street expects the electronic signature stalwart to post EPS of $0.81, implying a 1.2% decline, while revenue is expected to increase 5.5% to $748.92 million during the quarter.

For the first quarter, the company projected Q1 revenue to be between $745M and $749M, below the $757.2M estimate. First quarter subscription revenue to be between $729M and $733M, while billings are expected to fall within a range of $741M to $751M.

Seeking Alpha analyst Moretus Research believes that IAM is rapidly scaling, now more than 20% of new direct deals, and is set to drive both top-line growth and margin expansion beyond consensus expectations.

The analyst rated the stock as a Buy with a price target of $130, mainly due to faster-than-expected revenue growth and accelerating IAM monetization.

“Despite increased competition and premium pricing, DocuSign maintains a strong market position with a robust feature set and high popularity,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Khaveen Investments, rating the stock as Buy.

However, Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system maintain a Hold rating, reflecting cautious sentiment around competition and valuation.

On a year-to-date basis, DOCU has gained nearly 4 %, outperforming the broader S&P 500 Index, which rose 1.51% during the same period.

Over the last two years, DOCU has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 100% of the time.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have not seen any upward revisions, compared to14 downward moves. Revenue estimates have not seen any upward revisions versus 12 downward moves.

data.title: Grayscale files for AI infrastructure ETF with SEC
data.description:
Grayscale Investments has filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to launch the Grayscale Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure ETF, which plans to trade under the ticker GRAI. The ETF aims to provide investors with exposure to the VettaFi AI Infrastructure Index, a benchmark index of global companies that form the backbone of the artificial intelligence ecosystem.

Per the filing, the fund will carry a 0.59% annual operating expense ratio and focus on firms in six key AI infrastructure segments: Big data/analytics, cloud providers, data centers and connectivity, energy powering AI, network and security, and semiconductor and computing systems. 

To qualify for inclusion in the index, companies must have a minimum $200M market cap, $2M in average daily trading volume, and at least 20% of shares available to the public. Eligible stocks must be listed in developed or emerging markets as well such as the U.S., Japan, Germany, South Korea, and others. 

The ETF is designed to capture the growing demand for AI infrastructure as artificial intelligence becomes more deeply embedded in global technologies and services. If approved, GRAI will offer targeted exposure to companies enabling the next wave of AI advancement through infrastructure development. 

Moreover, if GRAI is to start trading it will likely find itself competing for market share with other AI focused exchange traded funds.

AI ETFs: (NYSEARCA:IVES), (NASDAQ:AIQ), (NASDAQ:BOTZ), (NYSEARCA:DTEC), (NYSEARCA:GINN), (NASDAQ:ROBT), (NYSEARCA:TECB), (NASDAQ:XT), (NYSEARCA:THNQ), and (NYSEARCA:CHAT).

data.title: UBS maintains its bullish stance on Netflix with a fat PT hike
data.description:
Analysts at UBS continued to stay bullish on streaming giant Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) and gave the stock a $300 price target hike. 

They believe secular trends and competitive dynamics remain supportive of the company's ability to drive stronger paid and ad-supported monetization and improve operating leverage. The stock remains a top pick under UBS' coverage of media stocks due to its favorable industry positioning and growing platform engagement.

"Solid viewership trends, a strong pipeline for new titles, and the favorable industry backdrop give us rising conviction in Netflix's ability to drive subscriber growth, monetization, and margin expansion in the coming years," UBS said in a June 3 research note.

UBS noted that consumption of general entertainment on linear is fading, but it still accounts for about 30% of all TV consumption in the U.S. across streaming and linear, which suggests that there are still several more years of viewership substitution in Netflix's largest and most mature market.

"With engagement on an upward trajectory and Netflix's share of total TV viewing still at ~8% in the U.S. (its largest and most mature market), we think there is a long runway ahead for potential share gains and viewership substitution from linear TV," the research firm said.

UBS also pointed out that Netflix Originals have accounted for a larger portion of top title consumption in the U.S. so far in 2025, at ~55% of tracked viewership vs. 49% for the same time last year.

NFLX has a "buy" rating and a PT of $1,450, up from $1,150, implying an upside of 19%. Stock is up over 37% so far this year, while the benchmark S&P index is up 1.5%.

data.title: VICI, with focus on dividend, return growth, still sees long-term prospects in Las Vegas Strip
data.description:
VICI Properties (NYSE:VICI) management stressed its focus on AFFO growth and total return growth in a presentation at an industry conference on Wednesday.

"Growing our dividend is a major focus along with AFFO growth," management said at the Nareit REITweek conference in New York.

When striking deals with partners, the REIT that focuses on experiential properties, "one of our principal investment criteria is making sure the operator is focused on the customer experience," said CEO Ed Pitoniak. "When we get nervous is when it’s hard to identify the core customer segment. And its ability to maintain same-store sales during downturns."

For the long term, VICI Properties (NYSE:VICI) sees significant opportunity in its properties on the Las Vegas Strip. It owns 1.7 miles of the 4 miles of frontage on the strip.

"Over the next 10, 20, 30, 40 years, development of that frontage represents the most significant opportunity for VICI," Pitoniak said. And it's not all in casinos. The company see opportunities in secular trends. Sports is one. The company is leaning into youth sports development, and wants to be a capital provider in that subsector.

For now, raising capital in the debt markets has its challenges. For example, there was a session this year when the yield on the 10-year Treasury swung 13 points intraday. "That's friggin' scary," Pitoniak said. "We are in the cost of capital business. When you don’t know what the cost is, it’s really hard to price," he said. The company, of course, does some hedging and has some floating rate debt.

"Our preference is to sell real estate," said Chief Financial Officer David Kieske. But when it comes to issuing debt, "we work hard to ensure our maturity ladder is staggered."

Its focus on its balance sheet has paid off. Earlier this year, Moody's upgraded VICI's debt to investment grade, resulting in an investment-grade rating at all three of the major ratings agencies.

"We’ve worked hard to get the balance sheet to give us the optionality to weather all storms," Kieske said.

With many specialty REIT stocks in the red on Wednesday, VICI (VICI) stock slipped 0.4% in midday trading.

data.title: HealthEquity surges 9% on quarterly beats, boosted guidance
data.description:
• HealthEquity (NASDAQ:HQY) is up ~9% in Wednesday morning trading after releasing fiscal 2026 Q1 results that beat on both lines and boosted its EPS outlook for the year.
  • For FY26, the company expects revenues of $1.285B-$1.305B. Consensus is $1.3B.
  • Non-GAAP net income per diluted share is projected at $3.61-$3.78. Consensus is $3.69.
  • In Q1, HealthEquity reported non-GAAP diluted EPS of $0.97 compared to $0.80 in the year-ago period.
  • The company ended the quarter (April 30) with cash and cash equivalents of ~$287.9M, compared to ~$296M on Jan. 31.

data.title: Abu Dhabi's IRH buys controlling stake in Alphamin in $366M deal
data.description:
Abu Dhabi-based International Resources Holding said Wednesday it agreed to acquire a 56% interest in Alphamin Resources (OTCPK:AFMJF), gaining access to one of the world’s biggest tin mines.

IRH said it will acquire nearly 719M Alphamin (OTCPK:AFMJF) shares from a unit of private equity firm Denham Capital at C$0.70/share for a total consideration of ~C$503M, or US$366M.

Alphamin (OTCPK:AFMJF) reported record tin production from the Bisie mine in the Democratic Republic of Congo of 17,324 tons for FY 2024, up 38% Y/Y, accounting for ~6% of total global supply of tin; production guidance for this year initially was set at 20K tons, but security concerns in the DRC earlier this year resulted in some interruptions to operations.

Denham Capital has been Alphamin’s (OTCPK:AFMJF) largest shareholder for more than a decade, making Bisie one of the few U.S.-linked mining projects in the DRC; President Donald Trump's top Africa advisor visited Kinshasa in April and said efforts to find a peaceful resolution were making "very good progress."

data.title: CoreWeave rises after results of largest-ever Nvidia Grace Blackwell GPU clusters
data.description:
Shares of CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) rose about 3% on Wednesday after the company, in collaboration with Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) and IBM (NYSE:IBM), delivered the largest-ever MLPerf Training v5.0 submission results for Nvidia's GB200 Grace Blackwell chips.

CoreWeave said 2,496 Blackwell graphics processing units, or GPUs, were running on CoreWeave's AI-optimized cloud platform.

The submission is the largest GB200 NVL72 cluster ever benchmarked under MLPerf, 34times larger than the only other submission from a cloud provider highlighting the large scale of CoreWeave's cloud platform for demanding AI workloads, according to CoreWeave.

CoreWeave noted that the submission achieved a breakthrough result on the largest and most complex foundational model in the benchmarking suite, Llama 3.1 405B, completing the run in 27.3 minutes.

CoreWeave's GB200 cluster achieved more than twice as faster training performance, compared to submissions from other participants across similar cluster sizes, according to the company.

"These MLPerf results reinforce our leadership in supporting today's most demanding AI workloads," said Peter Salanki, co-founder and chief technology officer at CoreWeave.

These results matter because they translate directly to faster model development cycles and an optimized total cost of ownership, according to CoreWeave.

For CoreWeave's customers, this could cut training time in half, scaling workloads efficiently, and training or deploying models more cost-effectively by using latest cloud technologies, months before their competitors, CoreWeave noted.

In April, CoreWeave — which went public in March — brought thousands of Grace Blackwell GPUs online, becoming the first cloud provider to make the new GPUs generally available at scale.

data.title: ECB June Meeting Preview: Rate cut expected as inflation falls below target
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) is expected to lower its deposit rate by 25 basis points to 2.00% at its June 6 meeting, as inflation in the eurozone slips below target and economic momentum remains weak. A sharp decline in both headline and core inflation in May has strengthened the case for monetary easing, with markets fully pricing in the rate cut.

Headline inflation for the region dropped to 1.9%, while core inflation fell to 2.3%, largely due to a notable slowdown in services inflation from 4.0% to 3.2%. This marks the first time inflation has dipped below the ECB’s 2% target since September 2023. With wage growth also easing and energy prices declining, the disinflationary trend appears to have solidified.

The ECB has already cut rates seven times since June 2023, and Thursday’s move is expected to be the first of potentially two further cuts this year. Economists expect the deposit rate to fall to 1.75% by year-end, as inflation remains subdued, and price pressures continue to fade.

However, uncertainty surrounding global trade tensions — particularly U.S. tariffs on European goods — complicates the outlook. While recent developments have temporarily eased concerns, the potential for retaliatory tariffs or further protectionist measures poses an upside risk to inflation. ECB officials are likely to tread carefully, avoiding firm commitments on the future path of rates.

ECB President Christine Lagarde is expected to adopt a cautious tone, emphasizing the need for a data-dependent approach amid ongoing external risks. Updated staff forecasts for inflation and growth will be closely watched, particularly in light of softening market-based inflation expectations.

While Thursday’s rate cut is highly anticipated, the ECB’s forward guidance and tone will be key in shaping market expectations for the rest of 2025.

data.title: Circle IPO set to price higher than marketed range after 25x oversubscription - report
data.description:
Stablecoin issuer Circle Internet Financial's (CRCL) upcoming initial public offering is likely to price above its marketed range of $27-$28 per share, after attracting demand over 25 times the shares on offer, according to a media report published Wednesday.

The company and selling shareholders would offer 32M shares at the top of the $27-$28 price range to raise $896M, according to its latest SEC filing. That's up from 24M shares at a $24-$26 range disclosed in an earlier filing.

The IPO is slated to price on Wednesday evening (New York time), Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

"Allocations of shares will be skewed toward investors with a strategy aimed at holding stock for the long term," the people added, noting that deliberations remain ongoing and Circle (CRCL) could still price the offering within the price range.

If priced at the top end of the range, Circle's (CRCL) market value would approach $6.2B, rising to roughly $7.2B on a fully diluted basis including stock-based comp and warrants, the article said. 

BlackRock (BLK), the world's largest asset manager, is reportedly planning to buy ~10% of the shares offered in Circle's (CRCL) IPO.
